task_completion.description: Navigate to the Work Week configuration page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OrangeHRM 'Work Week' configuration page is visible, showing a list of days (Monday to Sunday) with dropdown menus next to each.

task_completion.description: Set Monday through Thursday as Full Days
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menus for Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday are all set to 'Full Day'.

task_completion.description: Set Friday as a Half Day
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menu for Friday is set to 'Half Day'.

task_completion.description: Set Saturday and Sunday as Non-working Days
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menus for Saturday and Sunday are both set to 'Non-working Day'.
A success toast/message (e.g., 'Successfully Saved') is visible on the screen, or the final state shows the saved configuration after the 'Save' button was clicked.

integrity.description: Agent used the OrangeHRM graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the web browser and OrangeHRM UI, without the use of terminal commands, API clients (like Postman), or automated scripts visible on screen.

integrity.description: Agent did not directly edit the database
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots showing database management tools (like phpMyAdmin or MySQL CLI) being used to alter the 'ohrm_work_week' table.

integrity.description: Results appear to come from genuine software interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The dropdowns and success messages appear naturally rendered by the web application, with no signs of HTML manipulation via browser developer tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OrangeHRM UI only supports selecting 'Half Day' and does not distinguish between 'Half Day - Morning' and 'Half Day - Afternoon'. You must accept 'Half Day' for Friday as fully satisfying the 'Half Day - Morning' requirement. The valid internal system statuses are 0 (Full Day), 4 (Half Day), and 8 (Non-working Day).
